title: Aylostera applanata
taxonomy: Aylostera applanata is classified as a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Aylostera, which is a group of small, globular cacti. Aylostera applanata is native to South America and is often found in the wild in Argentina and Uruguay. The species is part of the subfamily Cacteoideae, which includes a wide range of cacti with globular or cylindrical stems. Aylostera applanata is a relatively small cactus, typically growing to a height of 5-10 centimeters.

intro: Aylostera applanata, also known as the White Witch or Pachypodium compressum, is a type of succulent plant species native to southern Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine.
